source: CIS AZURE KUBERNETES SERVICE (AKS) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF_updated_20250825_190647.json

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host network namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostNetwork flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's network namespace could access the local loopback device, and could access network traffic to and from other pods. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host network namespace. If you need to run containers which require access to the host's network namespaces, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostNetwork: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of hostNetwork containers Given that manually checking each pod can be time-consuming, especially in large environments, you can use a more automated approach to filter out pods where hostNetwork is set to true. Here’s a command using kubectl and jq: kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostNetwork == true) | "\(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name)"' OR kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostNetwork == true) | select(.metadata.namespace != "kube-system" and .metadata.namespace != "gatekeeper-system" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-arc" and .metadata.namespace != "azure- extensions-usage-system") | "\(.metadata.name) \(.metadata.namespace)"' When creating a Pod Security Policy, ["kube-system", "gatekeeper-system", "azure-arc", "azure-extensions-usage-system"] namespaces are excluded by default. This command retrieves all pods across all namespaces in JSON format, then uses jq to filter out those with the hostNetwork flag set to true, and finally formats the output to show the namespace and name of each matching pod.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostNetwork containers. Pod Security Policies and Assignments can be found by searching for Policies in the Azure Portal. A detailed step-by-step guide can be found here: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/policy-for- kubernetes Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostNetwork containers. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/policy-for- kubernetes 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-azure-policy 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/use-psa
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostNetwork: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.source: CIS AZURE KUBERNETES SERVICE (AKS) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF_updated_20250825_190647.json

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit can be performed using Kubernetes API capabilities, as outlined in the provided audit command (using `kubectl`). The command leverages API queries to list pods and filter based on the `hostNetwork` property, allowing for automated compliance checking.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_pod_security_host_network_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:
- Use Azure CLI commands to retrieve Kubernetes pod information for all namespaces.
- Specifically, the command would be: 
  ```
  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json
  ```
- The program will filter the output using JQ to identify any pods that have the `spec.hostNetwork` property set to `true`. The filtering should exclude specified namespaces such as `kube-system`, `gatekeeper-system`, `azure-arc`, and `azure-extensions-usage-system`.
- It will return a list of non-compliant pods that do not adhere to the policy against using the host network namespace.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- Pass: No pods found with `spec.hostNetwork: true` in the allowed namespaces.
- Fail: Presence of any pods with `spec.hostNetwork: true` in the allowed namespaces.

**Example API Endpoint or CLI Command:**
- CLI Command for automated checks:
  ```
  kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.hostNetwork == true and (.metadata.namespace != "kube-system" and .metadata.namespace != "gatekeeper-system" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-arc" and .metadata.namespace != "azure-extensions-usage-system")) | "\(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name)"'
  ```
